prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
1) English
Nice to meet you — I'm looking forward to working with you.
Note: Neutral, friendly professional register that signals openness and approachability in many English-speaking workplaces.

2) Spanish
Mucho gusto — un placer trabajar contigo.
Note: Uses the informal "tú" (contigo); many Spanish-speaking workplaces adopt this friendly tone, though some places/ages prefer the formal "usted."

3) French
Enchanté(e) de travailler avec vous.
Note: Uses the formal "vous"; polite and respectful—appropriate when you don’t yet know someone's rank or prefer to keep a professional distance.

4) German
Freut mich, Sie kennenzulernen — ich freue mich auf die Zusammenarbeit.
Note: Uses the formal "Sie"; customary to start formally in professional settings until invited to switch to "du."

5) Japanese
はじめまして。これからよろしくお願いいたします。
Note: Very polite (keigo) and humble—this standard workplace introduction shows respect and expresses a wish for smooth cooperation.
